text:
You just quit ChatGPT for Claude.

But you're lost on how to use it. Here is how:

1. Go to Claude (desktop app).
2. Pay for the monthly $20 plan.
3. Open the Cowork (it's the best).
4. You must create a "Cowork" folder on your PC.
5. To download my folder, go here: how-to-ai.guide.
4. Don't pay anything. It's free in the welcome email.
5. Then get my Notion with text files to download.
6. I made a guide for the next steps for Cowork.
7. Copy my entire setup: claude-co.work

---

1. Chat → just like ChatGPT.
☑ Make sure to use Opus 4.6.
☑ Always turn on 'extended thinking'.
☑ I barely use chat now. I only use Cowork.

2. Project → Chat, but separated by project.
☑ One Project per client. One per topic.
☑ Drop your files inside to keep Claude focused.
☑ I replaced Projects with Cowork. It does more.

3. Code → a revolution for developers.
☑ Developers code with Claude directly.
☑ It writes, debugs & ships. No copy-pasting.
☑ Skip it if you don't code. Cowork is your Claude Code.

4. Cowork → the only one that matters right now.
☑ Create 4 folders under one main folder 'Cowork':
☑ 'About me', 'templates', 'projects', 'Output. md'
☑ To download mine, go to how-to-ai.guide.
☑ Pay nothing. Get the link to download in the mail.

5. Skills → teach Claude once, reuse forever.
☑ Build a Skill for every task you repeat weekly.
☑ It's like a Project with permanent instructions.
☑ One good Skill replaces an entire SOP document.

6. Connectors → Claude inside your tools.
☑ Settings → Connectors → Browse → click "Add."
☑ Slack, Google Drive, Notion, Figma. 50+ tools.
☑ Claude reads, writes & acts inside them. It's free.

7. Plugins → pre-built workflows for your role.
☑ Customize → Browse plugins → Install. Also free.
☑ Type / to trigger commands like '/marketing'
☑ Customize each plugin to your company.

♻️ Repost this image to help others quit ChatGPT.

text:
This is the only Claude Cowork guide you need. 

(duplicate my exact folder, files, and prompts):

Step 1: Download the app (claude. com/ download)
Open the Cowork tab. Select the 'Opus 4.6' model.
Turn on Extended Thinking. Never change these.

Step 2: Build this exact folder on your laptop
One master folder: "Claude-Work."
4 subfolders: About me, Project, Template, Outputs.
Point Cowork to this folder. That's how it reads you.

Step 3: Create 3 context files inside ABOUT ME
about-me → what you do day-to-day. Not a resume.
my-voice → tone, phrases you hate, 3 writing pieces
my-rules → ask, plan, don't delete without approval.

Step 4: Or skip step 3. Download my own md files-

Go to how-to-ai.guide.
Subscribe for free. Don't pay anything.
Open my welcome email.
Hit the automatic reply button inside.
Download my .md files. Ready to upload.

Step 5: Set Global Instructions (once, forever)
Settings → Cowork → Edit Global Instructions.

Paste: "I'm [Name], [Role]. Read my files before every task. Ask questions before executing. Show a plan. Never delete without approval."

Set once. Runs every session. Never type it again.
Your prompts can now be 10 words long.

Step 6: Stop prompting. Start answering questions.
Claude generates clickable question form. Not text.
You select answers. No typing.

Prompt this for every task:
"I want to [TASK]. Read all files first. Ask me questions using AskUserQuestion before you execute. If something is off, generate new questions. Do not guess."

Step 7: Install your first plugin
Customize → Browse Plugins → Install.
Type / to see slash commands.
Marketing. Data. Legal. Sales. Start with 2.
This is why $285B in software stocks disappeared.

Step 8: Connect your tools
Settings → Connectors → Add.
Slack. Google Drive. Notion. Figma. 50+ more.
Claude reads your tools mid-conversation. 
No copy-pasting. Free on all plans.

The Don'ts:
✦ Write long prompts for every task
✦ Skip the folder and wonder why output is generic
✦ Dump 50 random files without structure
✦ Use Chat when you need Cowork
✦ Send deliverables without reading them first
✦ Forget Extended Thinking
✦ Expect Claude to read your mind without files
✦ Quit the desktop app mid-session

The Do's:
✦ Always select Opus 4.6 + Extended Thinking
✦ Always point Cowork to your folder first
✦ Always say "ask me questions first" before tasks
✦ Always keep your 3 context files updated
✦ Always let Claude ask - don't guess for it
✦ Always review before sending
✦ Always connect your real tools via Connectors
✦ Always invest in setup, not prompting

Now you know what to do first.

30 mins & folders to turn Claude into your coworker.

Most people will bookmark this and never do it.
You're not most people.

Full setup guide + my exact files: https://lnkd.in/dQGyX8rM

text:
How to make slides with Claude:

1. Task

☑ Define what you want & what success looks like:
"I want to build a pptx presentation so that [SUCCESS CRITERIA]."

No "make me a deck about X." 
↳ That's how you get 10 slides of nothing.

2. Context Files

☑ Upload your expertise and rules as files:
"First, read these files completely before responding: [filename.md] — [what it contains]."

Stop re-explaining your brand, your audience, your standards every single time. Put it in files.

3. Reference

☑ Show what you're aiming for. Upload an example.
"Here is a reference to what I want to achieve: [filename]."

No short prompts and hoping AI figures it out.

4. Research

☑ Claude searches before touching a single slide:
"Search the web using at least 5 varied searches (trends, data, expert opinions, case studies, counterarguments)."

It saves a structured research brief, organized by theme, with source URLs and key data points pulled out.

I like to add:
"Prioritize 2025–2026 sources. Flag anything where sources conflict or data is thin."

5. Slide Brief

☑ Turn research into a slide-by-slide outline:
"Each slide gets: a title, 3 key points, and any specific data/stats from the research that must appear on that slide."

No full paragraphs. Gamma will write the final text. Just give it enough structure and data to work with.

6. Rules

☑ Don't jump into making slides (yet):
"Do not generate slides yet." "Do not write full paragraphs."

A step-by-step approach takes longer, but makes (much) better slides. If you want to be faster, just go straight to Gamma.

7. Generate

☑ Pass the outline to Gamma as a presentation:
"Pass gamma-outline.md to Gamma as a presentation using textMode 'generate'. Use theme: [your brand theme name]."

Claude connects to Gamma for you. Like magic.

8. Theme

☑ Make sure to use your Gamma theme:
"Use theme: [your brand theme name]."

If you don't know how, I made a guide for teams at how-to-gamma.ai (scroll to the bottom).

After generating, review card by card:
→ Would I say this out loud?
→ Does this card earn its place?
→ Is the data right?

9. Alignment

☑ The prompt starts by asking YOU questions.
"Start by using AskUserQuestion to make sure you have enough context from me before researching."

Nothing starts until you're both on the same page. This replaces the old prompting era. 

I teach you how Claude works at claude-co.work.

Copy the full prompt template + download my personal .md files for Claude here: how-to-ai.guide.

Step 1: Subscribe for free → how-to-ai.guide.
Step 2: You will have two choices: free or paid.
Step 3: Choose the free tier. Don't pay for anything.
Step 4: Open your welcoming email (wait 30 sec).
Step 5: Access my entire prompt library & md files.
Step 6: Send this image to your team's channel.
Step 7: Read 2x newsletter per week (for free).
Step 8: Become the "AI guy" at work, forever.
Bonus: ♻️ Repost this to help others on Linkedin.

text:
ChatGPT looks like a toy in 2026.

I can't stop using the new Claude 'Cowork':

It reads your files. Directly from your computer.
No copy-paste. Just upload .md text files.
It sees everything. Then you just... talk to it.

"Read this file. Now write my newsletter on [topic]."

Most people prompt AI like this:
"Write something that sounds like me."

AI has no idea what "like me" means.
So it gives you the average. Then you rewrite again.

Here's what I do instead:

✦ I put myself in a text file. Literally.
✦ My opinions. My writing style. My "do nots."
✦ Download all my text files here: how-to-ai.guide
✦ Don't pay anything. Get the files in welcome mail.
✦ I drop the file in a folder. Claude reads it first.
✦ Now it writes like me. Thinks like me. Is me.

Here's (exactly) how you can do it too:

1. Open Claude desktop app
2. Go to Cowork tab (top left)
3. Select Opus-4.6 as your model
4. Create a new task
5. Copy my .md file prompt: https://lnkd.in/eF56s4i8
6. Select your folder with your .md file

From now, start every prompt with this:

"Read [your_name].md first. Then [what you need]."

✦ These are the 3 things I didn't expect:

1. 80% of my file is what I'd never write.

Not "I like short sentences."
But "I never use leverage, synergy, or start with In today's fast-paced world."

Taste is boundaries. Not preferences.

2. The file works everywhere.

Same .md file → ChatGPT Projects.
Same .md file → Gemini.
Same .md file → Grok.

I'm just a file now. My brain is portable.

3. My team now thinks like me.

I gave them the file.
Now they don't ask "how would Ruben say this?"
They upload. They prompt. Done.

I duplicated myself without a single meeting.

The uncomfortable truth is you're not unique.

Your "voice" is just patterns you never wrote down.
The moment you write them, they become transferable.
So transfer them. Before someone else does.

I wrote a (free) guide: https://lnkd.in/dQGyX8rM

document.title: How to get cheaper flights with AI:

text:
Stop using Google Flights to find cheap flights.

Copy paste these 5 prompts instead:

––––––––––––––––
Read the full guide on which AI I prefer using
(rather than ChatGPT): https://lnkd.in/gGux4N2Y
––––––––––––––––

1. Flexible Dates and Timing 

Prompt: "I’m flying from [origin] to [destination] around [month/year]. Find the cheapest dates to depart and return, based on pricing patterns.”

2. Nearby Airports 

Prompt: "Suggest alternate airports within 200km of [origin/destination] that might have cheaper flights, and estimate savings.”

3. Route Hacks (e.g., Hidden-City or Multi-City) 

Prompt: "Find routes where [destination] is a layover on a longer trip, making it cheaper than direct.”

4. Error Fares and Deals 

Prompt: "Hunt for mistake fares or deals from [region] to anywhere in [area].”

5. Comprehensive Comparison 

Prompt: "Act as a budget travel expert: Compare current prices for [route/dates] across Google Flights, Kayak, airline sites, and suggest the best option.”

Save this to never book a flight the same way again.

To go even deeper, copy paste more prompt here: 
↳ https://lnkd.in/gGux4N2Y

♻️ Repost this so they stop overpaying for flights.

activityOfUser.occupation: Founder & CEO at Artisan 🦄 | Building AI Employees | Hire Ava, the AI BDR Artisan

text:
10 months ago, we were barely using Salesforce.  Even though I've been a customer since 2005 (!)

Why?  Our sales team had dwindled to 3 people. And two of them wouldn't log in at all.

When we added Momentum.io to auto-log sales calls to Salesforce, one rep quit. Not because the job got harder. Because they didn't want their activity tracked.

Our CRM had become a ghost town.

🤖 Then ... we deployed 20 AI agents.  And Salesforce became the most important piece of software we run.

Here's what nobody warns you about when you go AI-first:

All your agents need somewhere to live. Somewhere to share data. Somewhere to avoid stepping on each other.

Without a hub, you get chaos. Your AI SDR reaches out to someone who just had a bad support interaction. Your inbound agent re-qualifies someone already deep in a sales sequence. Your agents conflict. Your data fragments.

We now run 4 AI SDRs — Artisan, Monaco, Qualified, and Salesforce Agentforce — all syncing back to Salesforce CRM:

→ Artisan: 15,000 messages in 100 days, 5-7% response rates across 3 outbound campaigns
→ Monaco: booked meetings with some of the biggest names in AI. From Day 1 after go-live.
→ Qualified (just acquired by Salesforce): powers our inbound "digital Amelia." 700K+ sessions. $3M+ closed. 71% of our closed-won sponsorship deals in one month came from AI-qualified leads. Historic average? 29-34%.
→ Agentforce win-backs: this one I'm almost embarrassed about.

After SaaStr AI 2025, we audited Salesforce and found ~1,000 people who filled out our sponsorship interest form and got ZERO human follow-up. Ever. We put Agentforce on it.

72% open rate. (Not a typo.) 10%+ response rate on "dead" contacts. Already closing deals from leads ghosted 6+ months ago.

Why such insane open rates? Because in this use case, Agentforce isn't doing cold outreach. It knows their full Salesforce history — past events attended, prior sponsorship levels, every interaction we've ever had. The emails feel like a relationship continuing, not a recovery sequence.

Cold email: 2-4% open rates. 
Artisan outbound: 5-7% response rates.  Excellent.
Agentforce on warm CRM contacts: 72% open rates.

Different categories entirely.

(And notice the pattern: Salesforce acquired Momentum. Salesforce acquired Qualified. They're not building from scratch — they're buying the best agents and pulling them into the platform.)

🚀 The CRM that becomes the hub for AI agents wins. The one that doesn't becomes a database you're overpaying for.

Going AI-first didn't make CRM less important.

It made us finally use it the way it was always meant to be used.

If you're deploying AI agents: don't skip the hub. Your agents need somewhere to live. 

..........

And want to see how all these AI Agents >truly< work?  And deploy them yourself -- for real?  They'll all be there at SaaStr AI 2026, May 12-14 in SF Bay!!

text:
How to duplicate your entire brain in AI (in 1 day):

Step 1: Download Claude (claude. com/ download)
Open Claude 'Cowork' tab. Select Opus 4.6 model.

Step 2: Copy-paste my interview prompt
From this guide: https://lnkd.in/eF56s4i8
It asks you 100 questions about how you think.

Step 3: Answer every question out loud
Use the Wispr Flow tool. Talk to yourself. Don't type.
Typing makes you edit. Talking makes you honest.

Step 4: Don't tell what you like. Tell what you reject.
"I like clarity" → useless.
"I never start with 'In today's world', I never use 'leverage' as a verb, I'd rather repeat myself than use jargon" → useful.
80% of your file should be what you'd never say.

Step 5: Get specific when Claude pushes back
"I keep it simple." → Claude asks: "Simple how?"
Answer: "3 sentences max per paragraph. No semicolons. No words over 3 syllables if a shorter one exists."
The specificity is the taste.

Step 6: Save this text file in .md format
One text file. Your entire brain.
Now build your brain file. Create "about-me .md."
To download mine, go here: how-to-ai.guide.

Step 7: Upload it to any AI.
From now on, start every prompt with:
"Read [yourname].md first."

What happens next:

→ AI writes like you on the first try
→ New hires get your voice instantly
→ You switch tools, your taste follows
→ You stop rewriting everything

What's inside your file:

✦ Your banned words (so AI never uses them)
✦ Your aesthetic crimes (what makes you cringe)
✦ Your hard nos (topics you won't touch)
✦ Your contrarian takes (so AI argues like you)
✦ Your writing style (how you open, close, pause)
✦ Your "always" vs "never" quick references

What changes after:

You stop saying "make it sound like me."
You stop rewriting 50% of every output.
You give the file to a new hire. They write like you.
You switch from ChatGPT to Claude. With context.
You update the file. Every AI gets the new version.
You are now portable.

I thought I was unique.
I'm just patterns I never wrote down.

So are you.

Master yourself so AI can master you.

Full process + my taste file: how-to-ai.guide.
